row_identity_policy_evaluation.title: Existing policy: conflict blocks append
row_identity_policy_evaluation.summary: Keep row_id as market identity and treat same row_id/different hash as a conflict.
cons: Hard to accumulate repeated intra-day observations.

row_identity_policy_evaluation.title: Observation key includes run_id or collected_at
row_identity_policy_evaluation.summary: Let repeated property/date observations have unique observation IDs.
pros: Avoids false conflicts for legitimate re-collection.
cons: Requires stable grouping key and view updates.

row_identity_policy_evaluation.title: market_identity_key plus observation version
row_identity_policy_evaluation.summary: Separate stable market identity from versioned observation records.
cons: Larger schema and append logic change.

row_identity_policy_evaluation.title: Skip benign metadata-only conflicts
row_identity_policy_evaluation.summary: Skip conflicts when only metadata/debug fields changed; block market-value changes.
pros: Resolves false conflicts without schema change.
cons: Still cannot store same-day market-value changes.

recommended_policy.short_term: Keep Option A blocking behavior for market-value conflicts; do not append conflicting B09X rows.
recommended_policy.medium_term: Implement Option B or C: add market_identity_key plus observation_id/run_id so repeated Booking observations are stored as time-series observations.
recommended_policy.b11x_recommendation: B11X remains blocked until row identity policy is fixed or conflicts are manually resolved.

b11x_blocker_status: B11X remains blocked until row identity policy is fixed or conflicts are manually resolved.
future_phase_plan: BOOKING-ID01X — Row Identity / Observation Model Design.
BOOKING-B10Z — Re-run append proposal with conflict policy applied.
BOOKING-B11X — Approved append only after conflicts are resolved.
